question_html:
Write down the appropriate preposition for the following sentences:

(a) The soldiers failed to rebel ____ the government.

(b) Education should be relevant ___the child’s need.

(c) Respond ____ your roll-call.

(d) I am thinking _____buying a new car.

(e) A strong opposite is vital ______ a healthy democracy.
solution_html:
(a) against (b) to (c) to (d) about/of (e) to

Marks Distribution:

For writing the appropriate prepositions; (0.5×5)=2.5 marks.

question_html:
Make sentences with the following group verbs:
‎break into, carry on, drop by, keep away, take up.
solution_html:
(i) Thieves tried to break into the shop last night.
‎(ii) Please carry on with your work while I make a call.
‎(iii) Why don’t you drop by sometime for a cup of tea?
‎(iv) Children should keep away from the construction site.
‎(v) He decided to take up painting as a hobby.

Marks Distribution:
‎(0.5×5) = 2.5 marks for making sentences with group verbs.

question_html: Write a paragraph on “Advancements in Biotechnology”.
solution_html:
Advancements in Biotechnology

Biotechnology has revolutionized various fields, from agriculture to medicine. Genetically modified crops enhance food security by increasing yield and resistance to pests. In medicine, biotechnology has enabled the development of life-saving drugs, vaccines, and advanced diagnostics. For instance, CRISPR-Cas9 technology has opened new avenues for gene editing, offering hope for curing genetic disorders. In Bangladesh, the application of biotechnology in agriculture and healthcare has immense potential to drive economic growth and improve living standards.

Marks Distribution:

For writing Title, “Advancements in Biotechnology”; 0.5 mark.

For writing the impact of biotechnology on agriculture;1.5 marks.

For writing the impact of biotechnology on medicine; 1.5 marks.

For writing about the potential of biotechnology in Bangladesh; 1.5 marks.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]
